content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

August 28, 1985


Carol Flynn
Rutan & Tucker
611 Anton Boulevard #1400
Costa Mesa, CA 92628

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. A-85-187

Dear Ms. Flynn:

Enclosed are the various opinions and advice letters you
requested. As we discussed on the telephone, the Political
Reform Act provides that when an official or his or her spouse has a
10% or greater ownership interest in a business entity, sources
of income to that business entity are considered sources of
income to the official. Government Code Section 82010(a). Thus
the clients and customers of a business are sources of income
within the meaning of Government Code Section 87103(c) to an
official who has a 10% or greater interest in the business
assuming the $250 threshold is met.

Please feel free to call me if I can be of further
assistance.

Sincerely,

Diane Maura Fishburn
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:plh
Enclosures

Law Office of

RONALD E. NULL                    F P P C
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

ASSOCIATE COUNSEL                 P.O. BOX 8025         Aug 26  9 02 AH'395
F. MACKENZIE BROWN, INC.                                    ANGELES (213) 587-1915
WARREN B. DIVEN                  FAIRBANKS RANCH PLAZA        ORANGE (714) 857-0464
THOMAS J. RUNYON                 RANCHO SANTA FE, CALIFORNIA 92067    RIVERSIDE (714) 359-5459
                                 (619) 756-5994


August 14, 1985


Robert E. Leidigh, Esquire
Legal Division
State of California Fair
 Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Re:  Request for Written Advice (Government Code §83114(b))
     Acceptance of Social Memberships in a Private Country Club by
     the City of Vista, California

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

We represent the City of Vista, California and in that capacity render City
Attorney services to said City.  We hereby request formal written advice
pursuant to Government Code §83114(b) concerning the acceptance of social
memberships in a private country club by the City of Vista.

Daon Corporation ("Daon") is the owner and operator of the Shadowridge
Country Club ("Club") within the City of Vista.  Initially, the Club was
operated as a public facility.  Daon has recently converted the Club to a
private membership facility.  Members of the City Council, the City Manager
and various City Department heads used the clubhouse facilities for official
activities during the time that the club was operated as a public facility.
Daon wishes to make a gift of social memberships to the City to allow
continued use of the facility by City Officials.

We have prepared a Resolution of Acceptance for adoption by the City
Council.  We believe the Resolution complies with the criteria set forth in
the Stone Opinion of the FPPC, reported at 3 FPPC Opinion 52.  We have
enclosed a copy of the Resolution for your review.

We are particularly concerned with whether or not the gift as currently
structured creates a conflict of interest for the members of the City
Council, the City Manager or the City Department heads and Daon.
Additionally, we inquire as to whether or not the members of the City
Council, the City Manager or the City Department heads must report the use
of the club as a gift.

Law Office of
RONALD E. NULL
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

Robert E. Leidigh
August 14, 1985
Page Two

Please provide to the undersigned the written advice as requested at your
earliest convenience.  Should you have any questions or need further
information, please call.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]

RONALD E. NULL

REN:lr

Encs.

CC:  Morris Vance

sections.question_synthetic: Two interconnected inquiries: (1) Whether a gift of social memberships in a private country club to a California city creates conflicts of interest for city officials, and (2) whether city officials must report the use of such club memberships as gifts.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The document references the Stone Opinion and indicates that the FPPC is providing written advice on whether the gift structure complies with conflict of interest requirements, though the specific FPPC conclusion is not provided in this excerpt.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two separate dated items: an August 14, 1985 incoming request letter (from Rutan & Tucker) and an August 28, 1985 FPPC response. The incoming request asks about country club memberships and conflicts of interest, while the FPPC response addresses income source reporting. The actual formal FPPC conclusion to the country club question is not fully visible in this excerpt. Confidence reduced due to incomplete final advice letter and mixed document components.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Two interconnected inquiries: (1) Whether a gift of social memberships in a private country club to a California city creates conflicts of interest for city officials, and (2) whether city officials must report the use of such club memberships as gifts.

CONCLUSION: The document references the Stone Opinion and indicates that the FPPC is providing written advice on whether the gift structure complies with conflict of interest requirements, though the specific FPPC conclusion is not provided in this excerpt.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter file containing a request from the City of Vista's attorneys regarding acceptance of social country club memberships as gifts and subsequent correspondence from FPPC staff counsel providing general guidance on income source reporting under the Political Reform Act.
